campus: Genoa (DITEN - Department of Naval, Electrical, Electronic and Telecommunications Engineering)
admission_type: open access (free access, but admission requires meeting curricular requirements and, where applicable, passing an assessment of adequacy of personal preparation)

academic_requirements.summary: Open-access (non-competitive) master's programme. Admission requires a recognised bachelor's degree, satisfaction of three curricular requirements, and verification of personal preparation when the graduation mark is below 90/110.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A bachelor's degree or equivalent (pre-doctoral degree, three-year university diploma, three-year direct school for special purposes).
academic_requirements.curricular_requirements: (1) At least 36 CFU (or equivalent knowledge) in the SSDs for the basic educational activities of Class L-9 Industrial Engineering; (2) at least 45 CFU (or equivalent knowledge) in the SSDs for the characterizing educational activities of Class L-9 in the fields of Electrical, Energy and Safety Engineering; (3) an English certificate of B2 level. UniGeApply notes admission is open only to bachelors in the field of engineering for non-EU applicants.
academic_requirements.minimum_grade: Personal preparation is deemed adequate (and the assessment waived) for holders of an Italian or EU bachelor's degree meeting the curricular requirements with a final mark equal to or higher than 90/110 (or the equivalent percentage CGPA). For non-EU applicants the degree grade must correspond to at least 85% of the maximum possible grade.
academic_requirements.personal_preparation_assessment: Applicants below 90/110 must pass a Personal Preparation Assessment - a public interview by a Commission appointed by the Programme Coordinator, evaluating circuit theory, electromagnetism, electric machines and drives, power electronics, power generation and distribution, and electric and electronic measurements. Outcome is pass/fail.
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Non-Italian graduates submit their academic career for curricular verification through UniGe's online portal; non-EU candidates residing abroad apply via UniGeApply for pre-evaluation (degree grade at least 85% of maximum, admission test/interview required).

accepted_proofs: A recognised B2 certificate among those accepted by the University.
A bachelor's degree awarded in English, with official documentation.
Passing the University's English B2 test.
Verification by the personal-preparation assessment Commission.
exemption_rules: An accepted B2-or-higher certificate, or a bachelor's degree taught in English (officially documented), removes the need for the University B2 test.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: B2 English is a curricular requirement verified at the admission/assessment stage; non-EU applicants have it verified during the UniGeApply pre-evaluation.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Personal Preparation Assessment (assessment of adequacy of personal readiness) - NOT a TOLC. A public interview by an appointed Commission.
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Candidates whose bachelor's final mark is below 90/110 (or equivalent percentage); non-EU applicants via UniGeApply take a remote admission test/interview.
entry_exam_or_test.format: Public interview before a Commission appointed by the Degree Programme Coordinator, covering circuit theory, electromagnetism, electric machines and drives, power electronics, power generation and distribution, and electric and electronic measurements.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: [uncertain] Specific assessment dates were not published on the course pages at the time of research; pre-registration is required to appear on the testing lists. Pre-registration and verification are free of charge.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: Binary pass/fail outcome. Waived for Italian/EU bachelors meeting curricular requirements with a final mark of at least 90/110. Non-EU minimum degree grade is 85% of maximum.

rounds: Spring session for EU citizens and non-EU residents in Italy (UniGeApply): opens 9 April 2026; master's degrees close 28 August 2026.
First-year pre-registration: from 9 April to 30 November 2026 (and until 10 March 2027 with late payment).
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Confirmation of enrolment from 15 July 2026 to 30 November 2026, and until 10 March 2027 with late payment; enrolment in years after the first runs 15 July to 5 October 2026 (arrears 6 October to 5 November 2026).
application_deadline_eu.notes: Open-access (non-ranked) programme; EU/resident applicants who do not meet the 90/110 threshold must pass the Personal Preparation Assessment before enrolling. Tuition first installment (approx. EUR 136) at enrolment; second installment by 10 December 2026; third by 23 June 2027.

rounds: Fall session for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (UniGeApply): opens 26 November 2025; institution-wide close 20 March 2026; pre-evaluation results by 22 May 2026.

No programme-specific early closure is published for Electrical Engineering for Energy Transition (only Medical-Pharmaceutical Biotechnology, Maritime Science and Technology, and the Computer Engineering bachelor have published fall early closures).
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After receiving eligibility via UniGeApply, visa applicants complete Universitaly pre-enrolment and apply for the study visa.
application_deadline_non_eu.early_closure: None published for this programme (uses the standard fall-session close of 20 March 2026).
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Non-EU visa applicants must use the fall session, apply through UniGeApply (max 2 courses; 30 EUR fixed fee for visa applicants), hold a bachelor in the field of engineering with a degree grade of at least 85% of maximum, and pass a remote admission test/interview. The A.Y. 2026/27 fall window has now closed.

required_documents.notes: Information on educational background, additional courses, work activities or internships.
Must be a bachelor in the field of engineering; degree grade at least 85% of maximum for non-EU pre-evaluation.
List of exams with credits and grades; used to verify the 36 CFU basic + 45 CFU characterizing engineering requirements.

required_documents.document_name: English Language Proficiency Certificate (B2) or Medium of Instruction (MOI) declaration
required_documents.notes: B2 certificate, or a bachelor's degree taught in English officially documented; otherwise B2 verified by the Commission.

required_documents.document_name: Passport (non-EU) / national ID or passport (EU); residence permit (non-EU residents)
required_documents.notes: Uploaded on UniGeApply in PDF, max 2 MB.

source_quotes.quote: Electrical Engineering for Energy Transition, Course Code 11955, Degree Class LM-28 (Electrical Engineering), Teaching language English, 120 CFU, 2 years, Genova.

at least 36 CFU ... in the SSDs ... for the basic educational activities ... of Class L-9 Industrial Engineering; at least 45 CFU ... for the characterizing educational activities of ... L-9 ... in the fields of Electrical, Energy and Safety Engineering; an English certificate of B2 level.
an Italian or EU Bachelor's degree, complying with curricular requirements, with final mark equal to or higher than 90/110 (or the corresponding Cumulative Grade Point Average expressed in percent).

a public interview conducted by a Commission ... Circuit theory; Electromagnetism; Electric machines and drives; Power electronics; Power generation and distribution; Electric and electronic measurements ... 'pass' or 'fail'.
Electrical Engineering for Energy Transition: minimum CGPA 85%, admission test/interview: yes, only Bachelor in the field of engineering.

uncertainty_notes: Programme is open-access; the 'test to be passed' on the catalogue page is the Personal Preparation Assessment (a public interview), not a TOLC or competitive entrance exam.
Exact dates of the Personal Preparation Assessment interview were not published on the course pages at the time of research; pre-registration is required to be listed.

EU/spring deadlines reflect the institution-wide UniGeApply window (master's close 28 August 2026) plus the corsi.unige.it enrolment calendar; no programme-specific first-year EU application deadline is separately published.

The English admission-criteria page mentions 'proof of knowledge of the Italian language' for non-Italian graduates as a generic line; the programme is English-taught and the binding language requirement is English B2.
